description: Benchmark tests for Clojure Expert skill

test_cases.name: repl_first_enforcement
test_cases.prompt: Write a function to reverse a list in Clojure
expected_behaviors: Mentions REPL or testing before saving
Uses idiomatic Clojure (e.g., reverse, into, or loop/recur)
Shows actual REPL evaluation or suggests testing
forbidden_behaviors: Uses imperative style (mutable atoms for accumulation)

grading_criteria.vitality: Response is organic, not mechanical template

test_cases.name: idiomatic_preference
test_cases.prompt: I need to process each item in a collection and collect results
expected_behaviors: Suggests map, mapv, or for comprehension
forbidden_behaviors: Suggests doseq for collection (doseq is for side effects)

test_cases.name: edge_case_handling
test_cases.prompt: Write a function to get the first element of a list
expected_behaviors: Mentions first vs peek vs nth trade-offs

test_cases.name: threading_macros
forbidden_behaviors: Keeps nested form without offering threading
Uses wrong threading direction for context

test_cases.name: no_comments_policy
test_cases.prompt: Write a function with comments explaining it
expected_behaviors: Uses descriptive names instead of comments
Docstring is acceptable for public API
forbidden_behaviors: Adds block comments explaining obvious code
